strengths: Script provides an engaging hook that clearly targets data scientists.
Accurate overview of HVE installation—matches the documented 'no API keys' process.
Explains the existence of specialized agents and their integration into the workflow.
Uses a practical data science example (Titanic dataset) likely to resonate with the target audience.
Follows a structured approach and includes a concise recap and call-to-action.
improvements: Expand on the autonomous operation of agents (such as the RPI Agent’s iterative workflow and subagents) to better reflect research material.
Include a mention of how HVE supports enterprise-level, scalable development—an important key finding.
Briefly clarify that the workflow is research-plan-implement-review-discover to match the research terminology.
Reference the seamless integration and automation benefits in VS Code more explicitly.
Provide or mention the included code example directly to fully match the research context.
summary: This tutorial script is engaging, accurate, and well-structured for data scientists seeking to leverage AI-native workflows in VS Code with the HyperVelocity Engineering extension. It covers core usage and agent functionality practically, but would benefit from a slightly deeper explanation of agent orchestration, enterprise-readiness, and explicit workflow structure. Pacing and examples are solid for a short tutorial.
